methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $270M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 1 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
REGENT Craft is a Series A transportation company founded by Billy Thalheimer and Mike Klinker, currently valued at $270M post-money on $60M raised to date. The broader Transportation sector contains 8 private comparable companies including Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, and FleetZero, though no public peers provide direct valuation anchors.

Stage-progression methodology is applicable here, using a 2.0x step-up multiplier from the last post-money valuation of $270M. This implies a $540M valuation at the next financing stage. Peer-median analysis is not applicable; while 4 same-sector peers exist, insufficient narrow-filtered comparables (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier) meet the threshold of 3+ datapoints required for reliable median calculation. Using an all-sector peer median would introduce unacceptable noise.

The triangulation analysis, constrained to a single applicable method, supports a valuation range of $459M to $621M, with a median of $540M. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression result. The primary uncertainty stems from the inability to cross-validate against peer multiples; a broader Transportation peer set at comparable stages would strengthen conviction. Secondary uncertainties include timing and terms of the next funding round, which will ultimately determine realized valuation.
